meaning: n.狭边钩齿粗木锯,双重不利的事物vt.用粗木锯锯,使受双重损失vi.利用一方打击另一方
examples: This divergence generates most of the whipsaw in a price chart.
After one such "whipsaw," Fecteau was asked by a guard: "Are you lonely now?"
Only Iceland which has suffered from a whipsaw currency has a larger deficit among developed nations.
This whipsaw movement extended throughout 2007 until finally breaking sharply to the downside in January 2008.
In other words, the boom partly reflects the cyclical trends that continually whipsaw agriculture.
